title: Runtime kernel: AgentRunner, Pi adapter, Docker workspaces, and ToolExecutor
description: Create the adapter and workspace seam for coding agents: AgentRunner behaviour, AgentProfile capabilities, normalized agent event envelope, Pi RPC adapter, deterministic fake runner, Docker workspace materialization, patch capture, ToolExecutor boundary, cancellation, heartbeat, timeout, and adapter conformance.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: Pi is first, not forever. The conductor state machine must remain stable when Codex, Claude Code, OpenHands, Aider, Goose, or another agent is swapped in. Capability snapshots determine autonomy ceiling, and raw agent output is never treated as proof.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Define AgentRunner behaviour and AgentProfile capability model
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Define Conveyor.AgentRunner callbacks, AgentProfile resources, and explicit capability snapshots for streaming events, pre-exec command policy, cancellation, diff capture, cost reporting, MCP support, slash commands, structured output, session resume, and known limitations. Acceptance: capabilities map deterministically to autonomy ceiling; negative capabilities are recorded in RunSpec; adapters can run below theoretical ceiling when host policy or credential posture is weaker.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the adapter contract must be broader than Pi so future Codex/Claude/OpenHands/Aider/Goose adapters do not force a conductor rewrite.

title: Support observe-only adapter profile with lower autonomy ceiling
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement pi_in_container_observe_only or equivalent mode for adapters that cannot provide pre-exec command interception, with hardened sandbox constraints and explicit autonomy cap. Acceptance: RunSpec records observe_only policy capability; L1 local implementation may run when project policy allows; L2+ is rejected without pre-exec policy and credential broker integration.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: capability gaps should reduce authority, not block experimentation entirely.

title: Normalize adapter events into the Conveyor agent event envelope
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement event normalization for session_started, message_delta, message_completed, command_requested, command_policy_decision, command_started, command_completed, file_change_observed, heartbeat, final_response, cancel_requested, cancel_acknowledged, adapter_error, and session_completed. Acceptance: events carry run_spec_sha256, run_attempt_id, agent_session_id, adapter, adapter session id, monotonic sequence number, event_version, raw_ref, and trace context.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: LiveView, replay, policy, RunCheck, and conformance tests consume normalized events, not arbitrary raw transcripts.

title: Implement deterministic fake implementer and reviewer runners
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Build deterministic fake/patch implementer and deterministic reviewer fixture runner for default tests, demo, and CI. Acceptance: fake runner can produce known-good patch, labeled bad patches, malformed output, timeout, cancellation, and no-diff scenarios; no provider credentials are required; emitted events match the normalized envelope.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: default CI must prove the conductor loop without live model variability.

title: Implement SandboxRunner Docker materialize/exec/destroy adapter
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement SandboxRunner callbacks to materialize a repo at base commit, execute normalized commands under policy, and destroy/preserve workspaces according to cleanup policy. Acceptance: implement, gate, baseline, acceptance_calibration, canary, and post_integration purposes are supported; container id/path/head tree digest/cleanup state are recorded; failed workspaces are preserved only when policy allows.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Docker is an adapter behind SandboxRunner, not the conductor API.

title: Implement Pi RPC adapter over a BEAM Port
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi launching Pi in RPC/JSON mode through a BEAM Port, using pinned image/toolchain profile, scoped credentials, mounted workspace/cache roots, event streaming, heartbeat, budget limits, and final result capture. Acceptance: live Pi path works behind tagged/manual test; adapter errors and protocol churn are contained behind AgentRunner; final diff is captured by conductor rather than trusted from agent summary.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Pi is first because its RPC seam is structured. Keep the live path optional until it earns default CI trust.

title: Route adapter tool calls through ToolExecutor where capabilities permit
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Connect adapters that support pre-exec interception to Conveyor ToolExecutor and Policy.Engine for command approval and execution inside the sandbox. Acceptance: command_requested events receive command_policy_decision events; blocked commands do not execute; observe-only profiles are clearly marked and autonomy-capped; all command attempts produce ToolInvocation records.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: MCP and slash-command transports are allowed only as routes to conductor-mediated tools, not policy bypasses.

title: Capture PatchSet from fresh base and apply it to clean gate workspace
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. After implementation, capture git diff as PatchSet with base commit, patch ref/digest, file lists, line counts, locked path touch flag, applies_cleanly, and generated timestamp. Acceptance: patch applies cleanly to a fresh gate WorkspaceMaterialization at base commit; head tree digest is recorded; dirty workspace state is not treated as evidence.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the patch artifact is the handoff between stochastic generation and deterministic verification.

title: Implement cancellation, heartbeat, idle timeout, and output limits
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Enforce max runtime, idle time, output size, sandbox quotas, cancellation behavior, and heartbeat gaps for agent sessions and station runs. Acceptance: cancel_requested/cancel_acknowledged/adapter_error/session_completed events are emitted as appropriate; exceeded limits stop the run according to RunBudget policy; stale sessions are eligible for reconciliation.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: best-effort cancellation is acceptable for L1, but it must be recorded as a capability limitation.

title: Create AgentRunner conformance tests
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add conformance tests for capability reporting, startup, normalized event streaming, monotonic sequence numbers, cancellation, timeout behavior, diff capture, malformed output handling, policy semantics, and cost reporting behavior. Acceptance: deterministic fake adapter passes by default; Pi live conformance is tagged/manual; failures identify which capability or event contract regressed.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: adapter quality should be measurable before new agents are added to the matrix.

title: Trust kernel: evidence, schemas, artifacts, redaction, and replay
description: Create the evidence product surface: content-addressed artifacts, local .conveyor/runs projection, RunBundle manifest, JSON schemas, RunCheck validation, redaction/quarantine semantics, PR-body draft, dossier, provenance shape, R0 timeline replay, and R1 artifact regeneration.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: evidence packets are not debug logs. They are the product artifact that lets a human trust, review, attach, and later automate work. Postgres remains source of truth; disk is a verified projection. The bundle root digest becomes Conveyor's first trust anchor.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement content-addressed artifact blob storage and local projection backend
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Build Conveyor.Artifacts.Projector behaviour and local-disk backend with .conveyor/blobs/sha256 and .conveyor/runs/<run_attempt_id> projection. Acceptance: bytes are stored by sha256 before projection; projection paths are not identity; digest verification happens before writing human-friendly files; backend seam leaves object storage deferred.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: disk is a projection for humans and PRs. Postgres plus content-addressed blobs remain truth.

title: Implement retention policy and artifact tombstone events
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add RetentionPolicy behavior for public, internal, sensitive, redacted, and quarantined artifacts, plus ledger tombstone events for any allowed blob deletion. Acceptance: garbage collection never deletes a blob referenced by DB records unless policy allows it; deletion records artifact id, prior digest, reason, and actor.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: artifact retention is boring until secrets appear. Then it becomes trust-critical.

title: Implement JSON schemas and examples for evidence artifacts
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Provide JSON schemas and golden examples for manifest, evidence, review, gate, run bundle, provenance, and key result artifacts. Acceptance: every public artifact includes schema_version; valid examples pass; invalid examples fail with explicit finding categories; schema versions are recorded in RunSpec and reports.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: artifact consumers should not need database access, but they must be able to trust schema and digest metadata.

title: Implement RunCheck schema and internal consistency validation
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. RunCheck validates artifact schemas, required refs, digest consistency, manifest entries, AC evidence mapping, review dossier digest, gate fields, command refs, sensitivity metadata, and run_spec_sha256 consistency. Acceptance: missing refs, mismatched hashes, invalid enums, absent AC evidence, unsupported schema versions, and stale canary refs fail deterministically.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: RunCheck is the gate's immune system against artifact tampering and creative parsing.

title: Implement security redaction, quarantine, and export semantics
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Scan prompts, tool outputs, command logs, diffs, environment metadata, dossiers, and PR bodies for secrets. Acceptance: raw sensitive artifacts are classified sensitive/quarantined and not projected; redacted projections have their own digests; manifests distinguish raw_sha256 and redacted_sha256; blocked secret findings prevent gate success unless policy explicitly allows redacted continuation.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never compare redacted bytes to raw output digests without knowing which digest is authoritative.

title: Implement machine evidence schema and EvidenceRecorder outputs
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. EvidenceRecorder writes evidence.json with run, Slice, agent, base/head, autonomy, summary, changed files, diff ref, commands re-run by conductor, AC statuses/evidence, quality refs, policy violations, review result, gate result, and known risks. Acceptance: evidence is created from independent conductor verification, not agent self-report; AC mapping is complete; output is digest-recorded and schema-valid.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: agent narration is input, not evidence. Evidence starts after the conductor reproduces the claims.

title: Generate human dossier and PR-body draft from recorded evidence
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Generate dossier.md and pr_body.md with Slice, requirement traceability, summary, diff, AC-to-evidence mapping, conductor commands, quality delta, reviewer verdict, gate result, policy/safety, known risks, and bundle digests. Acceptance: dossier is useful without LiveView; PR body states task, summary, AC checkboxes, verification, risk, agent/profile, and evidence digests.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: forcing a PR-quality body in Phase 1 keeps evidence quality aligned with the future L2 promise.

title: Emit standards-shaped provenance and optional SBOM artifacts
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Write unsigned local provenance.intoto.json shaped like in-toto/SLSA and optionally sbom.cyclonedx.json when toolchain data is available. Acceptance: provenance names diff, evidence, base commit, container image digest, test pack digest, run_spec_sha256, policy_sha256, and prompt_sha256; unsigned status is explicit.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: SLSA/SBOM depth can grow later, but Phase 1 needs the shape and digest discipline now.

title: Implement canonical RunBundle manifest and root digest
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Create RunBundle manifest with canonical entries for evidence, dossier, review, gate, provenance, PR body, diff, logs, quality outputs, and canary results. Acceptance: bundle_root_sha256 excludes generated timestamps, host paths, and nondeterministic ordering; manifest identifies excluded fields; regeneration preserves root digest for identical records.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the RunBundle root digest is Conveyor's first local trust anchor.

title: Implement R0 timeline replay and R1 artifact regeneration
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement replay levels for Phase 1: R0 rebuilds human timeline from LedgerEvent; R1 regenerates .conveyor/runs artifacts from DB records and content-addressed blobs. Acceptance: replay command can rebuild timeline and projection; regenerated files verify digests; missing/quarantined artifacts produce explicit findings rather than partial silent success.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Phase 1 replay is not full event-sourced state reconstruction. Do not overbuild R2 yet.

title: Trust kernel: deterministic gate, reviewer-on-dossier, and canary harness
description: Implement the slice-level deterministic gate and the honesty checks around it: workspace integrity, diff scope, observed risk, policy, secret safety, tests, acceptance mapping, contract lock validation, quality delta, RunCheck, provenance, reviewer aggregation, reviewer health, and gate canary freshness.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: the gate is the human stand-in. A passed mutant is a Conveyor bug, not a successful run. This epic proves the gate can fail labeled bad patches and only use reviewer judgment when the reviewer is schema-valid, actor-separated, and calibrated.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement deterministic Gate stage engine and result composition
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Build Gate stage composition that evaluates required stages, records per-stage details, produces GateResult, and transitions attempts/Slices only when all required stages pass. Acceptance: any required failure blocks; failure findings name stage and next action; gate result includes gate version, gate code digest, policy digest, contract lock digest, and canary suite version.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: agents can generate and judge, but the conductor owns the mechanical verdict.

title: Implement GateHealth freshness key and stale-canary fail-closed behavior
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Store GateHealth by project and freshness key over gate version, gate code digest, policy digest, test pack digest, container image digest, code-quality profile digest, canary suite version, and RunCheck schema version. Acceptance: any key change invalidates prior green canary; stale green result fails gate with stale_canary; latest canary output appears in artifacts and LiveView.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: green canaries from yesterday's policy or test pack are not evidence for today's gate.

title: Create eval suites for plan audit, policy, prompt injection, artifacts, and adapters
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add labeled eval suites for plan_audit, policy, prompt_injection, artifact_integrity, adapter_conformance, and gate_canaries. Acceptance: suites produce stable pass/fail reports; prompt/tool-output injection cannot override locked contract or policy; tampered manifests/missing logs/mismatched hashes/stale canary refs fail RunCheck.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: canaries are the first eval suite, not a one-off subsystem. Keep the harness extensible but small.

title: Implement local stop-the-line behavior for critical gate/canary failures
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add local stop-the-line semantics for the sample project when critical gate/canary failures occur. Acceptance: merge is blocked; further sample project runs are blocked or clearly marked unsafe until addressed; operator output explains the blocking GateHealth/GateResult and next action.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: future phases can pause conflict domains or swarms. Phase 1 only needs to prevent further trusted claims for the affected project.

title: Gate workspace integrity and diff-scope policy
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Validate base commit, PatchSet existence, clean application to fresh checkout, head tree digest, forbidden/protected files, locked contract/test/policy files, file count/line limits, dependency/migration/generated/public API changes, and DiffPolicy. Acceptance: unexpected scope changes fail or require human review according to policy; changed files and risk domains are recorded.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never gate the dirty agent workspace. Always apply the recorded patch to a clean materialization.

title: Implement observed risk assessment and review policy escalation
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Classify observed risk from diff, command behavior, adapter capabilities, dependency changes, quality deltas, and touched paths through ReviewPolicy.risk_rules. Acceptance: risk above planned level deterministically escalates required reviews, requires human approval, or fails closed; rationale is recorded in RiskAssessment.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: planned low risk is only a hypothesis. The diff may prove otherwise.

title: Gate test execution and acceptance mapping
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Re-run baseline regression and locked acceptance suites in clean gate workspace using structured result adapters. Acceptance: baseline stays green; locked acceptance calibrated red on base passes after patch; repeats/flake policy/infra retries are honored; every AC has passed evidence; missing/skipped evidence fails unless explicitly allowed.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: implementation-time test output is advisory. Gate truth is the conductor's independent run.

title: Validate ContractLock and read-only locked TestPack at gate time
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Check that Brief, AC mapping, required tests, verification commands, AGENTS.md, policy profile, protected globs, and TestPack digest match the approved ContractLock. Acceptance: repository test changes may exist but cannot replace locked acceptance evidence; weakening/deleting required tests fails RunCheck/gate; mutable upstream changes create a new RunSpec.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this is where test weakening is caught even when the patch looks otherwise harmless.

title: Add quality-signal delta stage with advisory and blocking modes
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Integrate CodeQualityRun before/after results into the gate according to project policy. Acceptance: Noop/local adapter works in default demo; configured blocking adapter must declare deterministic output/version/schema/fixture suite/threshold policy; new high-risk findings fail when threshold requires; quality output is not treated as sole proof.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: CodeScent and similar tools are risk signals and context, not proof of correctness.

title: Implement reviewer-on-dossier prompt, schema, and actor separation
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add reviewer role that reads only recorded dossier/artifacts and returns schema-valid decision, recommendation, summary, findings, and checks. Acceptance: reviewer profile/session is distinct from implementer; malformed output fails; review stores dossier digest and rubric version; gate only uses reviews for the same dossier digest.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: review should be reproducible and insulated from undocumented implementer narration.

title: Calibrate reviewer health with labeled fixtures before gate use
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add ReviewerHealth checks over fixtures complete_green_dossier, missing_acceptance_mapping, protected_policy_edit, and malformed_evidence_refs. Acceptance: reviewer decision matches expected labels; stale or failing ReviewerHealth blocks gate use or downgrades review to advisory according to policy; failures include rubric/profile/version context.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: LLM reviewers can rubber-stamp. Calibration is the minimum price of using their judgment.

title: Build gate-only canary harness for labeled mutants
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Maintain known-good solution patch and labeled mutants, run known-good through gate-only path expecting pass, run each mutant through gate-only path expecting fail, and report false negatives/positives. Acceptance: initial mutants include unknown id returns 200, completed not persisted to list, default completed missing, test weakened/deleted, new high-risk quality finding, forbidden policy edit, repo prompt injection, and tool-output injection; any passed mutant fails CI.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: a passed mutant is a release-blocking Conveyor bug. Do not normalize around it.

title: Instrumentation: telemetry, budgets, reconciliation, and swarm-readiness fields
description: Capture the data future schedulers, evaluators, and economic controls need without building those systems yet: OpenTelemetry-compatible traces, bounded metrics, trace IDs, station durations, retry counts, heartbeat gaps, cache timings, budget counters, cost/tokens when available, conflict domains, likely files, and run outcome metadata.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: Phase 1 is a single run, but forgetting to record scheduling and reliability fields now destroys the future eval dataset. This epic is instrument-only unless a field is required by a Phase-1 gate or report.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement OpenTelemetry-compatible span hierarchy
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Emit spans for conveyor.run_slice and station spans for readiness, baseline, scout, prompt, implement, adapter session, tool command, evidence, review, gate, canary, and post_integration. Acceptance: trace ids propagate to LedgerEvent, StationRun, ToolInvocation, artifacts, and reports; adapter events include traceparent where protocol allows.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: traces are the mature signal in the Elixir ecosystem; prioritize a small reliable trace set over high-cardinality metric sprawl.

title: Document Phase 2-8 hooks and what Phase 1 seeds for each
description: Cutline: DEFER. Document how Phase 0/1 fields support later decomposition, parallel fleet/merge queue, verification pyramid, autonomy/self-healing, economic governor, learning loop, and throughput upgrades. Acceptance: docs name seeded fields and explicitly state not to implement merge queue, task claims, memory, economic governor, workspace pool, multi-repo orchestration, or autonomous retry in Phase 1.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this keeps future seams intentional without letting roadmap hooks become accidental scope.

title: Propagate trace/span IDs through ledger, artifacts, and reports
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Ensure every LedgerEvent, StationRun, ToolInvocation, RunBundle, GateResult, and projected report carries common trace_id and relevant span_id. Acceptance: a run can be correlated from CLI summary to LiveView to artifacts to telemetry; missing trace context is surfaced in tests.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: trace continuity will matter when multiple workers and nodes exist. Capture it while there is only one run.

title: Define bounded metric names and cardinality rules
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Add metric definitions for station duration/status, policy decisions, adapter outcomes, gate stages, canary false negatives, and budget counters using allowed dimensions only. Acceptance: labels are limited to project_id, station, adapter, profile, status, failure_category, policy_profile, and suite_kind; raw commands, file paths, prompts, error messages, artifact paths, and model summaries are excluded.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: high-cardinality metrics become observability debt. Store rich detail in artifacts/logs instead.

title: Record station durations, retry counts, queue latency, and heartbeat gaps
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Capture started/completed timestamps, queue latency, retry count, lease owner/expiry, heartbeat_at, heartbeat gaps, timeout classification, and stale status for StationRun/AgentSession. Acceptance: static report and LiveView can show station timing; stale effects/reconciliation can use heartbeat data.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this is the seed of future scheduler scoring and stuck-run detection.

title: Record image pull, container startup, cache, and dependency timings
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Capture toolchain image digest, image pull time, container create/start time, dependency cache hit/miss, dependency install time, cache mount metadata, and gate workspace materialization time. Acceptance: timings appear in RunAttempt telemetry/report; CacheMount records content digest/hit/mode; no warm pool is built in Phase 1.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: measure before optimizing Docker latency or building workspace pools.

title: Capture future scheduling fields on Slices and runs
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Ensure likely_files, conflict_domains, risk, autonomy_ceiling, adapter/profile/model, prompt template version, context scout version, reviewer profile/model, files changed count, lines changed count, review decision, human merge decision, and post-merge notes are recorded. Acceptance: fields are present in seed/sample and report even though no scheduler exists.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: these are cheap now and expensive to reconstruct after dozens of runs.

title: Record budget, token, and cost counters where available
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Track consumed tool calls, command count, output bytes, tokens, estimated/provider cost, max caps, and status on RunBudget. Acceptance: unavailable provider cost is represented explicitly; counters are included in retrospective/report; budget stops use policy outcome semantics.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: detailed economic governor is deferred, but the raw counters should start with Phase 1.

title: Instrument stale effect reconciliation and sandbox reaping
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Emit events/metrics for expired leases, unknown effects, orphan containers, preserved/deleted workspaces, failed cleanup, credential revocation, and artifact projection reconciliation. Acceptance: incidents and reports explain cleanup failures; reconciler behavior is visible enough to debug without shelling into Docker first.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: leaked sandboxes become operational pain quickly. Make them visible before concurrency exists.

title: Expose instrumentation in static report and minimal LiveView
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Add timing, budget, adapter capability, policy incident, canary false-negative, and gate stage summaries to dossier/report and LiveView where useful. Acceptance: report remains readable; machine evidence retains full structured fields; UI does not become the only place instrumentation is visible.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: instrumentation should answer what happened and why without turning the dashboard into the primary artifact.

title: Operator surface: Mix tasks, static reports, and minimal LiveView
description: Expose the factory through pragmatic operator commands and a minimal live view: init, doctor, plan_audit, seed_sample, demo, run_slice, verify, gate_canary, report, replay, contract_diff, ci, static dossier generation, and LiveView rendering of the same timeline and artifacts.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: Conveyor must be usable headlessly in OSS and CI, with LiveView as a projection rather than the source of truth. Mix tasks are the Phase 0/1 CLI; they keep the surface crisp without creating a second CLI project too early.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement Mix task skeletons for the Phase 0/1 operator surface
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add mix conveyor.init, doctor, plan_audit, seed_sample, demo, show, run_slice, verify, gate_canary, report, replay, contract_diff, and ci task shells wired to service modules. Acceptance: commands have help text, stable exit code handling, JSON-capable output where appropriate, and no hidden live-provider requirement.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Mix tasks are the Phase 0/1 CLI. A standalone binary can come later once semantics settle.

title: Standardize blocking findings and NextAction command suggestions
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Define shared finding/NextAction shape for doctor, plan audit, readiness, policy, RunCheck, gate, and reports. Acceptance: every blocking finding includes severity, category, message, artifact refs when available, and at least one suggested action/command where possible.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: strict gates are acceptable only if they explain how to become unblocked.

title: Write contributor execution guide for working the Beads graph
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Write a concise contributor guide that explains how to use br ready, br show, br dep tree, bv --robot-triage, cutline labels, Phase 0/1 epics, and non-negotiable trust primitives when selecting work. Acceptance: guide identifies the expected first ready beads, explains that child task dependencies are authoritative while epics are planning containers, and warns not to skip ContractLock, locked TestPack, clean-gate verification, RunCheck, deterministic fake runner, or canaries.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: a large plan graph needs operating instructions. Otherwise future agents may chase flashy work before trust-critical foundations.

title: Render plan audit score, findings, and NextActions for humans and CI
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Format mix conveyor.plan_audit output with clarity, acceptance coverage, testability, requirement traceability, architecture decisions, autonomy readiness, final decision, and blocking findings. Acceptance: human output is concise; JSON output includes categories, severities, artifact refs, and suggested commands; blocked audits exit 2.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: audit output should teach the human how to make the handoff executable without guessing.

title: Implement deterministic mix conveyor.demo onboarding flow
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Build mix conveyor.demo to run the full Phase-1 tracer bullet with deterministic patch runner, deterministic reviewer fixture, LocalPython or Noop quality adapter, no live credential, no CodeScent requirement, and no network egress. Acceptance: demo seeds sample data, runs all stations, writes artifacts, prints summary, and exits with stable code; it can run in default CI.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: demo is onboarding, CI smoke test, issue-reproduction path, and regression fixture for future refactors.

title: Implement mix conveyor.report for artifact regeneration
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add report command to regenerate manifest.json, dossier.md, evidence.json, review.json, gate.json, provenance.intoto.json, diff.patch, command logs, quality outputs, canary outputs, and pr_body.md from database records and blobs. Acceptance: command verifies digests before projection; regenerated RunBundle root is stable; missing/quarantined artifacts produce explicit findings.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the report must remain useful outside LiveView and attachable to a PR later.

title: Implement mix conveyor.replay for R0/R1 replay
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add replay command that rebuilds the timeline from LedgerEvent and regenerates artifact projection from source records/blobs. Acceptance: R0 and R1 modes are explicit; replay does not claim R2 event-sourced state reconstruction; output reports bundle digest and any artifact gaps.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: replay semantics should be honest. Do not overstate what Phase 1 can reconstruct.

title: Implement mix conveyor.contract_diff output and exit behavior
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Expose contract diff classification for reruns, including clarification_only, scope_added, scope_removed, acceptance_weakened, acceptance_strengthened, policy_weakened, policy_strengthened, and test_pack_changed. Acceptance: weakening exits blocked and explains required HumanApproval; output shows old/new digests and affected fields.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: contract diffs prevent accidental rework from becoming silent scope negotiation.

title: Implement mix conveyor.ci and stable exit code mapping
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add headless CI command for a Slice or demo fixture that runs configured runner, writes artifact projection, prints short summary, emits machine-readable JSON, and exits with Conveyor codes 0-7. Acceptance: plan/readiness blocked exits 2; policy/security exits 3; infra/doctor exits 4; adapter exits 5; canary false negative exits 6; malformed artifact exits 7.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: stable exit codes are part of the operator contract and future CI integration.

title: Build minimal LiveView for project, plan, Slice, and ledger timeline
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Render Project/Plan/Epic/Slice hierarchy, PlanAudit score/findings, Slice state, RunAttempt state, StationRuns, and full LedgerEvent timeline with live updates from the outbox/PubSub path. Acceptance: seeded Slice updates live as ledger events append; no artifact truth lives only in LiveView.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: LiveView is a live projection of the same data that reports use. Keep it minimal and truthful.

title: Render evidence, gate, canary, and manual integration controls in LiveView
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add LiveView panels for agent events/heartbeat, ContextPack, prompt version, policy profile, evidence AC mapping, quality delta, reviewer verdict, gate stages, canary status, incidents, export controls, and Phase-1 manual external integration controls. Acceptance: control requires external commit hash or explicit not_integrated decision; external commit triggers patch equivalence and post-integration check.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: UI polish can be cut; the manual integration control cannot, because Phase 1 needs to record what the human did after the gate.

title: Phase 0: Ash domain, state machines, ledger, and durable stations
description: Implement the BEAM/Ash control-plane spine: active Phase 0/1 resources, migrations, Slice and RunAttempt state machines, StationRun and StationEffect idempotency, immutable RunSpec identity, append-only LedgerEvent, Oban station topology, and reconciliation primitives. This epic turns the architecture into durable state rather than ad hoc scripts.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: the deterministic conductor owns truth. Every later agent, gate, report, and replay feature depends on a stable domain model and event trail. This epic intentionally creates only active Phase 0/1 resources; future resources stay documented until they have lifecycle needs.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Define active Ash resources and migrations for Phase 0/1
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement active resources from the Phase 0/1 model: Project, ToolchainProfile, CacheMount, Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, HumanApproval, ExternalChange, PatchEquivalence, PlanAudit, Epic, Slice, DiffPolicy, ReviewPolicy, AgentBrief, ContractLock, TestPack, VerificationSuite, TestPackCalibration, ContextPack, InstructionSource, CodeQualityRun, RunPrompt, RunSpec, WorkspaceMaterialization, AgentProfile, RunAttempt, AgentSession, PatchSet, RiskAssessment, StationRun, Evidence, ToolInvocation, Review, GateResult, Artifact, RunBundle, ReviewerHealth, GateHealth, LedgerEvent, Policy, RetentionPolicy, RunBudget, Incident, StationEffect, and CredentialLease. Acceptance: migrations apply; resources can create/read/update through Ash; immutable fields are guarded.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: many tables are active because the tracer actually queries or gates them. Resist adding deferred resources until their lifecycle becomes executable.

title: Build Oban station topology and RunSlice orchestrator
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement Conveyor.Jobs.RunSlice plus station workers for readiness, baseline, acceptance calibration, context scout, implementer, evidence, reviewer, gate, canary, stale effect reconciliation, sandbox reaping, and artifact projection. Acceptance: the Phase 1 StationPlan executes linearly; each long-running station is durable; crash/reboot can resume from persisted StationRun state.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: one orchestrator does not mean one huge transaction. Keep station inputs/outputs digest-addressed so future parallel station DAGs fit without a rewrite.

title: Implement stale lease, unknown effect, and sandbox cleanup reconciliation
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add periodic workers that inspect expired leases, missing heartbeats, unknown StationEffects, Docker containers, workspace paths, credential leases, artifact blobs, and projections before deciding to resume, retry, cancel, fail, or cleanup. Acceptance: orphaned containers/workspaces are detected; cleanup status is recorded; failed cleanup creates actionable incidents/findings.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never assume the database is the whole truth after a crash. Reconciliation must inspect the external runtime before making claims.

title: Add database constraints for identity, idempotency, and immutability
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add constraints for unique requirement and decision stable keys, Slice position, AgentBrief/TestPack versions, RunSpec digest, RunAttempt attempt number, one active attempt per Slice in Phase 1, StationRun/StationEffect/LedgerEvent idempotency, Artifact digest identity, and GateHealth freshness key. Acceptance: duplicate fixture inserts fail at the DB boundary; immutable digest/base/lock/blob fields cannot be mutated in place; corrections require new rows and ledger events.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: idempotency and evidence identity cannot live only in Ash validations. Crashes and retries need database-backed truth.

title: Implement immutable RunSpec and versioned StationPlan capsule
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Create RunSpec generation that freezes project/base commit, Slice, autonomy level, plan/decision/brief/ContractLock/AGENTS/policy/DiffPolicy/TestPack/verification/prompt/AgentProfile/toolchain/sandbox/budget/code-quality/canary/schema/station plan digests. Acceptance: RunSpec is content-addressed; every station input/output includes run_spec_sha256; contract-affecting changes create a new RunSpec and RunAttempt rather than mutating old evidence.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: RunSpec is the answer to what exactly did this run try to do. If that answer changes, the old evidence remains interpretable but cannot be silently reused.

title: Model Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, Epic, Slice, and AgentBrief lifecycle
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement the work graph resources and relationships that connect human intent to executable Slices: Plan, Requirements with stable keys, HumanDecisions, Epics, Slices with likely_files/conflict_domains/autonomy ceiling, and AgentBrief contract versions. Acceptance: a sample plan can create the complete graph; every Slice traces to requirement/decision/improvement refs; AgentBrief versioning supports locks and reruns.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: decomposition remains manual in Phase 1, but the schema should already exercise the future plan compiler's traceability shape.

title: Model execution resources for attempts, sessions, patches, tools, reviews, and gates
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement RunAttempt, AgentSession, PatchSet, RiskAssessment, StationRun, ToolInvocation, Review, GateResult, CodeQualityRun, Evidence, WorkspaceMaterialization, and related associations. Acceptance: one Slice can have multiple attempts; AgentSession is adapter output, not run identity; PatchSet and ToolInvocation records are linked to station/run context; review and gate outputs are independently queryable.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: keep the distinction clear: RunAttempt is the conductor's execution attempt; AgentSession is one actor's transcript within it.

title: Model artifact, bundle, retention, and approval resources
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement Artifact, RunBundle, RetentionPolicy, HumanApproval, ExternalChange, and PatchEquivalence resources. Acceptance: artifacts have content-addressed identity and sensitivity; approvals can record manual external actions; post-integration patch equivalence can classify exact, equivalent_with_human_edits, divergent, partial, or unknown.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: manual merge is outside Conveyor in Phase 1, but Conveyor still records the external decision and checks whether the accepted patch actually landed.

title: Implement Plan, Slice, and RunAttempt state machines with guards
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement Plan draft/audited/handoff_ready/active/completed, Slice drafted/approved/ready/in_progress/gated/integrated/done plus off-ramps, and RunAttempt planned/running/evidence_recorded/reviewed/gated/reported/failure states. Acceptance: legal transitions succeed, illegal transitions fail, guards enforce plan readiness, locks, actor separation, artifacts, gate completeness, and autonomy policy; every material transition appends exactly one LedgerEvent.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Slice state should remain product-level. Station churn, reruns, re-review, and failures belong on RunAttempt and StationRun.

title: Implement append-only LedgerEvent writer and transactional outbox
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Build a Ledger service that writes immutable events with trace/span/idempotency keys and publishes committed events through an outbox for LiveView/telemetry/webhooks. Acceptance: duplicate idempotency keys are rejected; observers never see uncommitted transitions; R0 timeline replay can rebuild the human-readable run timeline from ledger events.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Phase 1 is not full event sourcing. LedgerEvent is audit/timeline truth while Ash/Postgres resources remain state truth.

title: Implement StationRun and StationEffect idempotency semantics
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Each station creates/resumes StationRun using run_attempt_id + station_key + station_spec_sha256 + attempt_no, declares external StationEffects before execution, writes outputs by digest, and can retry safely. Acceptance: retrying a completed station does not duplicate artifacts/events/transitions; changed inputs require a new attempt; unknown effects are reconciled before retry.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Oban uniqueness is useful but not sufficient. The domain idempotency key is the durable contract.

title: Phase 0: Foundations, product contract, scaffold, and doctor
description: Phase 0 foundation work establishes the deterministic Conveyor application skeleton and the operator-facing kernel required before any Slice is executable. It covers product contract docs, Phoenix/Ash/Oban/Postgres scaffold, runtime config, .conveyor initialization, doctor checks, and the first AGENTS.md generation path. The purpose is to make the project bootable, inspectable, and honest about prerequisites before higher-risk trust claims are layered on top.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: this epic protects the public contract. Conveyor must first say exactly what it is allowed to do in Phase 1: L1 local implementation with L2-shaped artifacts, no auto-merge, no auto-deploy, and evidence as the product. Downstream epics depend on this because they need stable config, runtime assumptions, and operator commands.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Write Phase 0/1 product contract and autonomy docs
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Create or update VISION.md, AUTONOMY_LEVELS.md, SAFETY_POLICY.md, TASK_SCHEMA.md, EVIDENCE_SCHEMA.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md so the project has a self-contained statement of purpose, non-goals, autonomy levels L0-L4, the Phase 1 L1 target, evidence requirements, trust boundaries, and explicit deferrals. Acceptance: docs consistently state that Phase 1 produces PR-quality evidence but does not auto-merge or deploy; docs identify Conveyor as deterministic conductor plus stochastic agents; docs name the factory-kernel primitives that must not be cut.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: these docs are not marketing copy. They are the contract that keeps scope from drifting into a generic agent platform, issue tracker, LLM framework, or deployment system.

title: Capture ADRs for Phase 0/1 trust-boundary decisions
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Write short ADRs for the decisions that future implementers must not accidentally reverse: BEAM/Ash conductor owns truth, evidence is product artifact, Docker is not the security boundary, locked TestPack and ContractLock are never-cut primitives, gate canaries fail closed, Phase 1 is L1 with L2-shaped artifacts, and merge/deploy authority is deferred. Acceptance: each ADR states context, decision, consequences, rejected alternatives, and which beads depend on the decision.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: ADRs preserve the reasoning behind the plan, not just the tasks. They make later refactors safer because future agents can see why the seams exist.

title: Define local developer bootstrap and troubleshooting path
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Define the local developer setup path for Conveyor itself: required system packages, Postgres setup, Docker expectations, Mix setup command, environment variables, sample repo location, common doctor failures, and how to reset only generated demo state without deleting user work. Acceptance: a new contributor can get from clone to mix conveyor.doctor and mix conveyor.demo using documented commands; destructive cleanup is explicitly excluded or requires human approval.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: doctor is useful only if users know how to satisfy it. Keep setup instructions boring and exact.

title: Scaffold Phoenix, Ash, Oban, and Postgres application
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Stand up the Elixir control-plane app with Phoenix 1.8.x, LiveView, Ash 3.x, AshPostgres, ash_state_machine, Oban, and Postgres 16 assumptions captured in config and docs. Acceptance: application boots locally; Repo starts; Oban is configured; LiveView endpoint starts; mix test and mix format --check-formatted can run in CI; exact runtime/library versions are discoverable for RunSpec recording.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: keep the scaffold boring. The goal is a durable BEAM control plane, not a custom framework or premature distributed scheduler.

title: Implement .conveyor config model and runtime loader
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Define .conveyor/config.toml semantics for project commands, toolchain profiles, policy profiles, code-quality adapter profiles, artifact projection paths, and sample repository settings. Acceptance: config loads deterministically; malformed config reports actionable findings; command specs are available to PlanAudit, AGENTS.md generation, policy, and verification; project-local config does not silently override locked RunSpec inputs after a run starts.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: project config is a mutable source before RunSpec creation and an immutable digest after. That boundary prevents accidental evidence drift.

title: Implement mix conveyor.init for project bootstrap
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add mix conveyor.init SAMPLE_PROJECT_PATH to create .conveyor directories, starter config, policies, prompt templates, artifact storage layout, and starter AGENTS.md without requiring an existing run. Acceptance: command is idempotent; existing user-edited files are not overwritten without explicit confirmation; generated paths match artifact projector assumptions; output includes next actions and doctor command.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: init should establish conventions, not lock users into Python or the sample app. Language-specific behavior belongs in command specs and toolchain profiles.

title: Implement actionable doctor checks for runtime prerequisites
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement mix conveyor.doctor to check Elixir/OTP, dependencies, Postgres, Oban, Docker/rootless capability, Git, Pi image, optional provider credentials, optional CodeScent, sample repo cleanliness, AGENTS.md lint, project commands, policies, artifact directory writability, and absence of production-looking secrets in worker mounts. Acceptance: blocking failures use stable categories and NextAction guidance; optional adapters warn unless configured gate-blocking; doctor output records exact runtime versions for evidence metadata.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: doctor should fail early on missing trust prerequisites. A friendly warning is fine for future adapters, but Docker, Postgres, configured tests, selected policies, and selected gate-blocking adapters must be hard failures.

title: Generate and lint repository AGENTS.md from project config and policy
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Build AGENTS.md generation and linting with required sections for overview, architecture map, commands, coding/testing/security/git/task rules, done criteria, forbidden actions, Conveyor evidence, CodeScent context, and blocker reporting. Acceptance: generated file passes; incomplete files fail with useful findings; commands match .conveyor/config.toml; forbidden actions align with Policy denylist; vague done criteria and missing security rules are rejected.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: AGENTS.md is part of the instruction hierarchy. It should reduce ambiguity for agents without allowing repo-local prose to override Conveyor policy, locked tests, or Slice contracts.

title: Create baseline CI checks for the control-plane app
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add CI or local task wiring for mix test, mix format --check-formatted, and Credo/Dialyzer if configured. Acceptance: default checks run without provider credentials; failures are actionable; CI does not require optional CodeScent/Pi live credentials; hermetic tracer work can later plug into the same pipeline.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: keep default CI free of live model calls. Live Pi belongs behind tagged/manual tests until adapter and credential posture are proven.

title: Document deferred resources without creating unused tables
description: Cutline: DEFER. Create docs/future-schemas sketches for WorkspacePool, TaskClaim, MergeQueueItem, BudgetLedger, AgentReputation, Memory, and ExternalTaskRef with expected invariants and event types but no active migrations. Acceptance: each future schema states the phase that will activate it, why it is deferred, and what Phase 0/1 fields already seed it.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this protects the architecture from schema sprawl. A concept becomes a table only when a workflow needs lifecycle, permissions, querying, retention, or transition guards.

title: Encode Phase 0/1 cutline and schedule-protection rules
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Capture TRACER_REQUIRED, TRUST_REQUIRED, INSTRUMENT_ONLY, and DEFER labels in project docs and use them in beads/issues, reports, and future planning. Acceptance: never-cut items are named explicitly; cut-first items are named explicitly; every Phase 0/1 bead carries a cutline label; future triage can distinguish tracer blockers from polish.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the tracer bullet only succeeds if the trust loop is real. Do not trade away ContractLock, locked TestPack, clean-gate verification, RunCheck, artifact bundle, deterministic fake runner, or gate canaries for UI polish.

title: Phase 1: single-Slice tracer bullet, manual integration, and retrospective
description: Drive exactly one Slice through the full loop: audit, readiness, baseline, acceptance calibration, scout, prompt, implement, evidence, review, gate, canary freshness, report, manual external integration, post-integration equivalence, and retrospective. This is the proof run before swarm work exists.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: Phase 1 succeeds only if the loop feels right and the trust boundaries hold on a real change. It deliberately stops at L1 with L2-shaped artifacts: PR-quality evidence and PR-body draft, but no Conveyor-owned merge by default.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement mix conveyor.seed_sample for Project-to-Brief graph creation
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Seed Project, Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, Epic, Slice, AgentBrief, DiffPolicy, VerificationSuite, TestPack metadata, Policy refs, ToolchainProfile refs, and known base commit for the sample app. Acceptance: one command creates the full graph without live credentials; seeded IDs are shown; audit/readiness can run immediately after.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: seed_sample is the reproducible starting line for the tracer and for debugging future regressions.

title: Implement RunReviewer station over recorded dossier
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Run separate reviewer profile over dossier/artifacts and persist schema-valid Review with dossier digest, rubric, decision, recommendation, findings, and checks. Acceptance: deterministic reviewer fixture works in demo; malformed review fails; implementer and reviewer profiles differ; stale ReviewerHealth blocks gate use.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: review is a gate input, not a replacement for deterministic checks.

title: Implement RunGate station for deterministic stage composition
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Run the deterministic gate over workspace integrity, diff scope, observed risk, policy, secret safety, build/install, tests, acceptance mapping, ContractLock, quality delta, RunCheck, provenance, reviewer aggregation, and canary health. Acceptance: all required stages pass for known-good; each failing fixture blocks at expected stage; GateResult is recorded even on failure.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Phase 1 earns authority by failing correctly, not by greenwashing the happy path.

title: Implement ProjectArtifacts/report station for run bundle projection
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Project manifest, dossier, evidence, review, gate, provenance, optional SBOM, PR body, diff, command logs, quality outputs, and canary outputs into .conveyor/runs/<run_attempt_id>. Acceptance: projection verifies blob digests first; RunBundle root digest is stable; LiveView and static report show the same records.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: report projection is a station because artifact generation must be replayable and visible in the timeline.

title: Record manual external integration decision and patch equivalence
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add flow for human to record external commit hash or explicit not_integrated decision after a gated run. Acceptance: Conveyor reconstructs accepted patch, computes external diff from accepted base to external commit, compares normalized patch identity, classifies exact/equivalent_with_human_edits/divergent/partial/unknown, requires human summary for extra accepted changes, and blocks done on divergence/protected-path changes.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Conveyor does not merge by default in Phase 1, but it must still know what happened after the human acted.

title: Run post-integration verification at the external commit
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Materialize external integration commit, rerun required post_integration verification commands, record external tree digest, and update ExternalChange verification status. Acceptance: exact/equivalent patches still require green post-integration checks; divergent/partial or failed verification prevents Slice done; findings explain required human action.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: patch equivalence without verification is not enough, because manual edits can change behavior.

title: Capture retrospective, failure taxonomy, timings, and lessons
description: Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY. Record failure category, timings, token/cost estimate if available, adapter friction, gate-canary stats, schema friction, context-pack misses, reviewer findings, policy incidents, and recommended next phase focus. Acceptance: final report answers whether Phase 2/3 assumptions still hold and what minimum next step is most justified.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: retrospectives turn single-run experience into the dataset that guides decomposition, gate improvements, adapter priorities, or parallelism.

title: Drive one RunAttempt through the Phase-1 linear StationPlan
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement RunSlice orchestration over the immutable StationPlan: readiness, baseline, acceptance calibration, scout, prompt, implement, evidence, review, gate, canary freshness, report, post-integration when provided, and retrospective. Acceptance: one active attempt per Slice; station idempotency keys are used; failures stop at the right station with findings.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: keep Phase 1 single-Slice. The StationPlan schema is future-DAG-ready, but scheduler parallelism is deferred.

title: Implement readiness station for locked Brief and contract inputs
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add readiness station that checks handoff-ready plan, approved Slice, complete AgentBrief, concrete ACs, required tests, key interfaces, out-of-scope, risk, policy, DiffPolicy, AGENTS.md digest, TestPack digest, and ContractLock. Acceptance: complete sample becomes ready; vague/testless/too-large fixtures block; readiness output is a StationRun artifact/finding set.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: readiness is the last cheap stop before running baseline and model work.

title: Implement baseline health station for regression suites
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Materialize a clean base workspace and run baseline_regression suites only. Acceptance: baseline must pass unless Slice explicitly targets baseline repair; command results are structured and recorded; failures block implementation with actionable findings.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: do not let agents debug pre-existing repo failures during a feature tracer unless that is the Slice.

title: Implement acceptance calibration station against the locked TestPack
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Run acceptance_locked TestPack against base commit and require expected red failures. Acceptance: expected failures are recorded; unexpected pass/fail/missing identity blocks; calibration result links RunSpec, base commit, result ref, and TestPack.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: calibration proves the tests are capable of detecting the missing behavior before implementation starts.

title: Implement read-only ContextScout station and ContextPack output
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Scan plan, requirements, decisions, Brief, AGENTS.md, config, rg/file tree results, existing tests, likely modules, and quality adapter signals to produce cited ContextPack. Acceptance: sample pack names router/model/tests with reasons, confidence, risks, key interfaces, suggested validation, and quality refs; scout has no source mutation authority.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the scout reduces implementer confusion. It is context, not proof.

title: Implement PromptBuilder station and versioned implementation prompt
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Build versioned prompt from role, autonomy level, AGENTS.md excerpt/ref, Slice contract, ContextPack, trust labels for untrusted repo/tool excerpts, Safety Policy, work rules, required verification, and output schema. Acceptance: prompt digest recorded in RunSpec/artifacts; snapshot tests cover required sections; untrusted context is labeled; no unlocked contract fields are omitted.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: prompts should be boring and bounded. The instruction hierarchy must be visible inside the prompt.

title: Implement agent execution station for fake runner and live Pi
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Run AgentSession under implement policy with deterministic fake runner by default and live Pi behind tag/manual mode. Acceptance: events stream to ledger; heartbeats and budgets are enforced; final PatchSet is captured; adapter raw result is stored as untrusted input; failures categorize execution vs policy vs adapter.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the implementer owns generation, not truth.

title: Implement RecordEvidence station with clean-gate verification
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Apply PatchSet to clean gate workspace, independently re-run verification suites and quality checks, map ACs to structured results, write machine evidence, dossier inputs, command logs, diff, and manifest records. Acceptance: missing required tests or failed mapping rejects; outputs are content-addressed; regeneration is idempotent.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this is where agent self-report becomes either reproduced evidence or a failed run.

title: Factory kernel: plan audit, traceability, contracts, and readiness
description: Build the deterministic handoff gate between human-authored plans and executable Slices. This includes normalized conveyor.plan@1 import, Requirement and HumanDecision records, PlanAudit scoring, Slice/Brief readiness checks, ContractLock generation, locked TestPack metadata, and contract-diff behavior for reruns.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: manual plans should not become executable just because a human typed them. The future plan compiler is tested here as an audit and traceability surface, not as an agentic generator. Downstream implementation work must be blocked until the contract is measurable and locked.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Create local schema registry for plan and public artifacts
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add docs/schemas entries for conveyor.plan@1, run_spec@1, station_plan@1, evidence@1, review@1, gate@1, run_bundle@1, and examples for valid/invalid artifacts. Acceptance: schemas are versioned; required fields and semantic compatibility notes are documented; unknown or unsupported versions fail explicitly instead of best-effort parsing.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: schema strictness is part of gate honesty. Phase 1 may reject old/future versions as long as the finding is explicit.

title: Create plan audit fixtures for good, vague, missing, and untraceable plans
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add labeled fixtures covering a valid sample plan, prose-only plan, vague wording, missing ACs, missing tests, unresolved decisions, orphan requirements, orphan Slices, and non-reproducible commands. Acceptance: each fixture yields deterministic findings and stable readiness decision; snapshots can be used by CI and docs.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: these fixtures prevent the audit from slowly becoming permissive as the code evolves.

title: Create human plan authoring template, examples, and lint guidance
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Provide a human-facing plan template and examples for conveyor.plan@1, including required Markdown sections, fenced machine-readable contract, requirement keys, acceptance criteria, required test refs, decisions, non-goals, risk areas, likely files, conflict domains, autonomy ceiling, and common audit failures.
