comments.text: Future-self note: strict audits are user-hostile without authoring guidance. This bead makes the plan gate teachable.

title: Implement normalized conveyor.plan@1 import from Markdown or sidecar YAML
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Parse a sidecar conveyor.plan.yml or fenced conveyor-plan@1 block from Markdown, validate it against schema, persist normalized contract, compute contract_sha256, and retain narrative source refs. Acceptance: prose-only plans may lint but cannot become handoff_ready; normalized contract includes project, goal, non-goals, requirements, ACs, verification commands, decisions, and slices.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Markdown explains intent; the normalized contract is what Conveyor hashes, locks, audits, and passes downstream.

title: Implement deterministic PlanAudit scoring and findings
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement audit checks and output for clarity, acceptance coverage, testability, requirement traceability, architecture decisions, autonomy readiness, risk policy, likely files, and reproducible verification commands. Acceptance: good sample plan reaches handoff_ready; missing acceptance/tests/decisions/traceability/risk policy produces blocking findings with NextActions and rerun commands.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: false positives are acceptable early if findings are concrete. Silent ambiguity is not.

title: Enforce requirement, acceptance, test, and Slice traceability
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Enforce that every Requirement has a stable key/source section/status, every AC maps to requirements, every required test maps to ACs, every Slice maps to a requirement/decision/bug/improvement, and open requirements block handoff_ready. Acceptance: orphan requirements and orphan Slices fail audit; deferred/out_of_scope statuses are explicit and reported.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this is the miniature of the later plan compiler. The audit is simple but strict; it should not invent missing mappings.

title: Model explicit human decisions, approvals, and contract changes
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Persist HumanDecision records for architecture choices and scope exclusions, and HumanApproval records for contract changes, acceptance weakening, policy weakening, and external integration. Acceptance: contract-affecting changes require an explicit decision/approval rationale; approvals reference artifact digests where relevant.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: weakening a test or policy is not a normal retry. It is a new contract and needs a human reason attached to it.

title: Implement AgentBrief contract resource and readiness checks
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Store AgentBrief versions with current behavior, desired behavior, key interfaces, out-of-scope, risk, ACs, required tests, verification commands, non-goals, lock metadata, and contract digest. Acceptance: complete Brief can transition Slice to ready; vague/testless/too-large Brief blocks with findings; readiness checks produce stable categories.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the implementer receives a bounded execution contract, not authority to reinterpret the plan.

title: Generate and invalidate ContractLock records
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement ContractLock creation over plan contract, brief, ACs, required tests, TestPack, verification commands, AGENTS.md, policy, and protected path globs. Acceptance: lock digests are stable; any change to contract-affecting inputs invalidates future attempts; old locks remain usable for interpreting old evidence.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: ContractLock is one of the never-cut trust primitives. Without it, the gate cannot know whether the agent weakened the assignment.

title: Model locked TestPack metadata and acceptance refs
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Persist TestPack version, source ref, content digest, required test refs, AC refs, mount path, runner command specs, result adapter, and lock metadata. Acceptance: locked packs can be mounted read-only; required test identities are preserved; repository test edits cannot replace locked acceptance evidence.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: implementers may see tests, but gate truth comes from the locked pack outside the editable workspace.

title: Implement contract_diff classification for reruns
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add contract diff logic that compares an old RunAttempt contract against a new plan or Brief and classifies changes as clarification_only, scope_added, scope_removed, acceptance_weakened, acceptance_strengthened, policy_weakened, policy_strengthened, or test_pack_changed. Acceptance: acceptance/policy weakening blocks automatic rerun and requires HumanApproval; any contract-affecting change creates a new ContractLock, RunSpec, and RunAttempt.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: second attempts must be clear about whether they retry the same contract or execute a changed one.

title: Quality system: ExUnit coverage, CI, eval suites, and conformance tests
description: Build the tests that keep the deterministic core honest: unit and integration tests for plan audit, policy, state machines, station idempotency, artifact projection, RunCheck, schemas, redaction, gate canaries, prompt injection, artifact tampering, adapter conformance, and hermetic tracer CI.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: Conveyor is a verification product. Default CI must exercise the full loop through fake/deterministic runners without live provider credentials, while live Pi remains tagged/manual. The test suite is what lets future autonomy increase with evidence instead of vibes.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: TDD deterministic core services with focused ExUnit coverage
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add tests for Readiness, PlanAudit, Traceability, PromptBuilder, RunCheck, EvidenceRecorder, Gate, Policy.Engine, Artifact projection, ContractLock, and TestPack calibration. Acceptance: deterministic services have focused unit coverage; edge cases use fixtures; tests do not require live providers.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the control plane must be more predictable than the agents it supervises.

title: Keep live Pi tests behind explicit tag/manual gate
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add @tag :live_agent tests for Pi adapter startup, event streaming, patch capture, timeout/cancel, and basic sample run with provider credential. Acceptance: not run by default CI; skip output explains required credential/image; failures do not block hermetic demo unless explicitly requested.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: live agent volatility should not hide deterministic conductor regressions.

title: Test clean-container reproducibility between agent and gate environments
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add test proving that a diff that appears green in the agent container must pass in the clean gate container or the run fails. Acceptance: fixture with hidden ambient state fails; gate workspace materialization is independent; ToolchainProfile image digest is recorded in evidence.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: if the gate cannot reproduce success, the run failed, regardless of agent claims.

title: Maintain project quality commands for code changes
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Ensure repository quality commands are documented and runnable: mix test, mix format --check-formatted, Credo/Dialyzer if configured, plus any sample-app pytest checks. Acceptance: default developer workflow and CI invoke the same commands; optional heavy checks are clearly labeled; no live-provider dependency in ordinary quality gates.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this bead is the landing checklist for code changes; it keeps product-kernel work from outrunning the tests.

title: Test state machines and ledger event emission
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Verify legal transitions, illegal transitions, transition guards, ledger event idempotency, and exactly-one event emission for material transitions. Acceptance: Plan/Slice/RunAttempt states match design; blocked transitions explain guard failure; duplicate idempotency keys are rejected.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: hidden state changes destroy replay and operator trust.

title: Test station idempotency, retries, and changed-input behavior
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Cover retrying completed stations, retrying failed/unknown effects after reconciliation, avoiding duplicate artifacts/events/transitions, and creating a new attempt when inputs change. Acceptance: station idempotency keys behave as domain contract; Oban uniqueness is not the only protection.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: crashes happen. The test suite should make retries boring.

title: Add snapshot tests for plan audit findings
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Snapshot valid, prose-only, vague, missing AC, missing tests, unresolved decision, orphan requirement, orphan Slice, risk without policy, likely-files missing, and non-reproducible command cases. Acceptance: findings remain stable and actionable; legitimate wording changes update snapshots intentionally.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: audit strictness should change deliberately, not by accidental parser drift.

title: Test policy enforcement and secret redaction/quarantine
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add fixtures for dangerous commands, env access, network attempts, protected path edits, fake credentials in logs/prompts/diffs/dossiers, and redaction policies. Acceptance: violations create incidents/block as configured; redacted exports use separate digests; sensitive raw artifacts are not projected.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: policy and redaction are gate inputs, so regressions must fail before demo confidence is claimed.

title: Test artifact schemas, RunCheck failures, and compatibility handling
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Cover public artifact schema validation, missing refs, mismatched manifests, invalid enums, missing AC evidence, unsupported schema versions, future minor versions without declared compatibility, and old major versions without migration. Acceptance: failures use explicit finding categories like unsupported_schema_version or schema_validation_failed.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never parse creatively in the trust path.

title: Run gate canaries in CI and fail on false negatives
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Wire known-good and enabled mutant set into default CI or hermetic tracer CI. Acceptance: known-good passes; every enabled mutant fails for expected or stricter reason; any false negative exits with Conveyor code 6; false positives are release-blocking if they prevent useful tracer proof.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: gate honesty measurement begins before parallelism.

title: Implement labeled eval harness for audit, policy, injection, artifact, and adapter cases
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Build eval runner and reports for plan_audit, policy, prompt_injection, artifact_integrity, adapter_conformance, reviewer fixtures, and canary suites. Acceptance: eval cases are versioned fixtures/artifacts in Phase 1, not active tables; results are stable and visible in reports; failures block where configured.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: keep eval storage simple until workflows need querying/retention as resources.

title: Run full hermetic tracer in default CI without live credentials
description: Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add default CI path that uses deterministic patch runner, deterministic reviewer fixture, Noop/local quality adapter, no CodeScent requirement, no network egress, and no live provider credentials to drive one RunAttempt through all stations. Acceptance: artifacts are produced; gate passes for known-good; replay/report works; failures use stable exit codes.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this is the regression shield for the whole product kernel.

title: Trust kernel: safety policy, sandbox, command grammar, and credentials
description: Implement explicit safety controls around every executable station: Policy profiles, normalized command grammar, denylist/allowlist evaluation, environment and network policy, Docker sandbox defaults, CredentialLease handling, incidents, budget stops, and command recording. Docker is necessary but not sufficient; policy defines intent.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: the agent adapter is not the security boundary. Conveyor must enforce policy through sandbox constraints and ToolExecutor-mediated commands, and it must record violations as evidence. This epic gates any credible L1 run and prevents later autonomy from growing on an unsafe base.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Document and test the Phase 0/1 threat model
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Turn the threat model into docs and fixtures for malicious repo content, malicious tool output, policy evasion, test weakening, secret exposure, supply-chain drift, artifact tampering, reviewer rubber stamps, gate false negatives, internal DB probing, and host escape/overreach. Acceptance: every threat class has at least one Phase-1 doctor check, test, canary, or policy fixture; docs identify primary defenses and residual risks.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this prevents Docker from becoming a false comfort. The agent, repository, and tool output are untrusted inputs.

title: Add safety and secret checks to doctor and readiness
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Extend doctor/readiness to verify policy profiles, denylist configuration, sandbox support, artifact projection permissions, no production-looking secrets in configured mounts, no Docker socket/home mounts, selected adapter capability/autonomy compatibility, and selected gate-blocking tool availability. Acceptance: failures are actionable and map to stable exit code 3 or 4 as appropriate.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: policy failures should be found before spending model time whenever possible.

title: Normalize command specs, paths, env, network, and roots before execution
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement the structured command grammar with executable, argv, cwd, env keys, stdin ref, network, write roots, read roots, and timeout. Acceptance: raw shell strings are rejected unless explicitly allowed; cwd/symlinks/write roots are normalized; executable path resolves inside the container; only configured command families are allowed; policy decision is recorded before execution.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: policy should evaluate intent before process launch. String denylisting is only defense-in-depth after normalization.

title: Implement policy profiles, allowlists, denylists, and escalation behavior
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement explore, implement, verify, release, and maintenance profile semantics with minimum denylist coverage for destructive filesystem commands, dangerous git commands, force pushes, chmod/chown outside workspace, pipe-to-shell installers, sudo, credential access, production DB URLs, unapproved package installs, unapproved network, and deploy/release/publish commands at L0-L2. Acceptance: fixtures block expected commands and create incidents where severity requires it.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: false positives are tolerable in Phase 1; silent bypasses are not.

title: Route executable station commands through ToolExecutor and Policy.Engine
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement ToolExecutor as the only component allowed to execute policy-mediated station commands, producing ToolInvocation records with command spec, profile, cwd, env keys, network mode, exit code, timing, output refs, output digest, policy decision, and status. Acceptance: allowed sample commands run; forbidden commands are blocked pre-exec when adapter capabilities permit; observe-only adapter modes are marked and capped.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: adapter-reported command execution is not trusted unless it passed through ToolExecutor.

title: Implement Docker sandbox defaults and capability checks
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Define Docker sandbox defaults: non-root user, rootless preferred, no privileged containers, no Docker socket, no host home mount, read-only mounts for contracts/policies/.conveyor, read-write workspace only, no-new-privileges, seccomp/AppArmor where available, resource/output/time limits, and network none by default. Acceptance: doctor reports host capabilities; selected policy profile fails if required sandbox constraints are unavailable.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the sandbox constrains blast radius even if the agent ignores every instruction.

title: Implement station-level network and egress policy
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Enforce explicit network defaults per station: scout none except optional quality endpoint, implement none except provider API when required, verify/gate none unless approved bootstrap, canary none. Acceptance: sandbox cannot reach Conveyor Postgres, ledger, or internal services; approved egress is external-host-only; unapproved network attempts are recorded and block according to policy.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: a compromised sandbox must not be able to probe or mutate the conductor.

title: Implement CredentialLease and scoped provider credential handling
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add CredentialBroker and CredentialLease records scoped to one run or station, exposed only through allowed env keys, never written into prompts/artifacts/logs, and revoked on cancellation, violation, or completion. Acceptance: missing live credential is a warning unless live agent mode is selected; injected env keys match policy; leases expire/revoke; secret scanner catches accidental exposure fixtures.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: raw provider secrets should not enter worker containers unless no safer adapter mode exists.

title: Implement RunBudget and non-progress exhaustion stops
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Track wall clock, idle time, tool calls, command count, output bytes, repeated command failures, same-file rewrites, no-diff progress time, tokens, and cost where available. Acceptance: budget exhaustion records consumed counters and structured findings; repeated identical failures, output flooding, heartbeat-without-progress, and no patch progress trigger policy-controlled stop rather than ordinary agent failure.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: cost and loop control are safety features, not just billing features.

title: Create incident handling for policy, secret, and sandbox violations
description: Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement Incident creation and Slice/RunAttempt outcome behavior for policy violations, sandbox failures, secret exposure, dangerous commands, and suspicious environment/network access. Acceptance: incidents include severity, category, description, evidence refs, and status; high-severity violations stop the run and move Slice to policy_blocked or failed according to policy.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: policy findings need operational consequences. A logged warning is not sufficient for trust-critical violations.

title: Phase 1: sample FastAPI app, human plan, Brief, and locked acceptance tests
